title: $1,000 in Google at IPO (2004)
description: What $1,000 invested in Google (now Alphabet) on August 19, 2004 — its IPO date — would be worth today. Google raised $1.67B in a Dutch auction IPO at $85/share, famously allowing any investor to participate. Many considered the price too high; it was not.

splits.note: Created GOOG Class C shares alongside GOOGL Class A. Effective doubling of share count.

dividend_note: Google/Alphabet did not pay dividends for most of this period. Alphabet initiated its first-ever dividend in Q1 2024.
educational_note: Google's Dutch auction IPO was unusual — it was designed to let small investors participate at the same price as institutions. The $85 IPO price seemed expensive vs peers; with hindsight it was extraordinarily cheap.
